title: gilia cotulifolia
intro: Gilia cotulifolia, also known as the purple gilia or cotuella, is a perennial plant native to the western regions of North America. It is a member of the Polemoniaceae family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Gilia cotulifolia can be prepared for medicinal use in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to respiratory issues.
